components.task_instruction: You are given a piece of text about a potentially controversial or debatable topic, such as politics, policy, or social issues. Your job is to determine the stance the text takes with respect to the main implied issue, based on explicit or implicit cues. Examine whether the opinion or sentiment is expressed as support, opposition, or neither; pay close attention to sarcasm, indirect language, and when the stance is aimed at a side in a debate or only satirical/critical of individuals, not the issue itself. Only assign a stance if the text clearly supports or opposes the issue itself—do not infer a stance based solely on generalized criticism, jokes, or unrelated commentary. Assign just one category per text.
components.label::favor: The text expresses support for the main idea, proposal, or policy itself, either directly or by approving arguments, actions, or outcomes aligned with the core position—even if this is done by challenging its opponents (e.g., defending abortion rights by criticizing anti-abortion stances). Assign 'favor' only when the primary stance is for the main issue, not just praising individuals or criticizing opponents without reference to the core topic.
components.label::against: The text expresses opposition to the main idea, proposal, or policy itself, either by criticizing it directly or by arguing against its key claims, supporters, or consequences—even indirectly (e.g., opposing abortion by rejecting pro-choice arguments). Assign 'against' only when the main intent is to argue against the core issue, not solely to attack individuals or complain about unrelated problems.
components.label::none: The text does not clearly express a stance, opinion, or sentiment for or against the topic, or is unrelated or neutral.
components.boundary_rules:
Assign only one category per text.
Output only the label: 'favor', 'against', or 'none'.
